observations.summary: The physical difficulty Dan experiences in trying to say the word 'Mother' with heavy tongue and sluggish fingers foreshadows his impending loss of voice and the transformation of his truth into noise, symbolizing a deeper internal or spiritual crisis.

source_evidence.quote: He lifted an unsteady hand. The fingers thick, sluggish. He tried to say the simplest word—Mother—and the tongue went heavy in his mouth. Not silent. Not yet. But dense, forewarning, a cold thing lodged where certainty used to live. The terror was not the physical sensation. It was what it foretold: to be a speaker whose truth was only ever heard as noise.

observations.target_hint: Dan's later loss of voice and spiritual transformation as described in the narrative context and master compendium.
